title: brassica saxatilis subsp. humilis
intro: Brassica saxatilis subsp. humilis, also known as wild cabbage or sea cabbage, is a subspecies of wild cabbage native to the Mediterranean region, characterized by its low-growing, rosette-forming habit. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it beneficial for treating digestive issues, skin problems, and respiratory infections. The health benefits of Brassica saxatilis subsp. humilis include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve immune function, and support overall well-being. Herbal preparations of the plant include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to make teas, salves, and other topical applications.
